channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Neighbor's Vengeance: Water Pipe Sabotage and Crossbow Threats
item.description: This article details a prolonged conflict between two neighbors, Amanda and Richard Collins, and their landowner Francis. The dispute began when Francis sabotaged the water supply by cutting the pipe,...

item.title: Brandon Declares State of Emergency Amid Crisis
item.description: Brandon, Manitoba, has declared a state of emergency following a significant crisis that has impacted the city. The declaration, made by local authorities, comes amid concerns over public safety and i...

item.title: Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen Shares Admiration for Kenyan Celebrities and Warns Against Cyberbullying
item.description: Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen has sparked online reactions after revealing the Kenyan celebrities he admires most during a TikTok Q&A session. The CS highlighted rapper and content cre...

item.title: Former DA Leader's PR Firm Linked to Tender Opportunities in Tshwane and Joburg
item.description: The article details allegations that former DA leader Tony Leon, through his PR firm Resolve Communications, sought to secure tender contracts from the Tshwane and Johannesburg metropolitan municipali...

item.title: Wildfire near Thessaloniki prompts evacuations and emergency response
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire near Thessaloniki, Greece's second-largest city, has forced evacuations due to strong winds making containment difficult. The fire broke out in a mountainous ravine around 8:30p...

item.title: Adeyemi Dismissed as Governor Adeleke's Aide Over Alleged Fraud and Misconduct
item.description: Prince Matthew Adeniyi Adeyemi, accused of operating a fictitious Federal Government agency, was once appointed as Senior Special Assistant on Protocol to Osun State Governor Ademola Adeleke in 2023. ...

item.title: Alibaba Bans Employee Use of Claude Code Over Security Concerns
item.description: Alibaba Group has reportedly implemented a policy prohibiting its employees from using Anthropic's programming tool Claude Code, effective July 10, 2026. This decision follows Anthropic's existing res...

item.title: France advances to World Cup quarters with narrow win over Paraguay
item.description: France secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory against Paraguay in scorching conditions at the World Cup, with Kylian Mbappe scoring the decisive penalty in the 70th minute. The match, played in temperature...

item.title: Senate Prepares for Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial with Security Measures and Public Access
item.description: The Senate has initiated a registration process for the public to attend the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, which is set to begin on July 5, 2026. Senate Secretary Renato Bantug Jr....

item.title: MP Leonard Yegon Ranked Top-Performing in South Rift and Bomet
item.description: Hon. Leonard Yegon, the Member of Parliament for Bomet-Konoin Constituency, has been ranked as the best-performing MP in South Rift and Bomet County according to the latest Infotrak survey. The rankin...

item.title: Nuclear Waste, Disarmament, and Environmental Concerns in Global News
item.description: The article discusses a wide range of topics centered around nuclear issues, environmental concerns, and global activism. Key points include the importance of nuclear waste management, efforts toward ...

item.title: Ray Emordi Returns to Acting After Taking Break for Music
item.description: Nollywood actor Ray Emordi has decided to return to acting after a four-month hiatus, marking a significant shift from his earlier decision to focus on music. The 35-year-old actor initially announced...

item.title: Senate Proceeds with Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial Amid Accountability Concerns
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian urged the public to closely monitor the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, emphasizing its role in ensuring institutional accountability. The House of Represe...

item.title: B.C. Lions secure first CFL victory with 36-24 win over Edmonton Elks
item.description: The B.C. Lions secured their first win of the CFL season with a thrilling 36-24 victory over the previously undefeated Edmonton Elks at the Apple Bowl. James Butler led the charge, rushing for 135 yar...

item.title: New Ebola treatment trial starts in eastern Congo amid ongoing outbreak
item.description: In eastern Congo's Ituri province, researchers have launched a critical study evaluating two potential treatments for the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola virus. The WHO-supported trial, led by Congolese an...

item.title: Investors Show Interest in Low-Fuel Mining Operations
item.description: Mining operations that prioritize low-fuel consumption are gaining traction as investors seek sustainable and cost-effective solutions. The global shift toward reducing fossil fuel dependency is resha...

item.title: Zambales Fisherfolk Honor Arbitral Ruling Anniversary
item.description: Hundreds of fisherfolk in Zambales, Philippines, gathered to commemorate the anniversary of a significant arbitral ruling that impacted their community. The event, held on July 5, 2026, marked the 10t...

item.title: Morogoro School Garage Converted Back to Commercial Use After Failing Safety Standards
item.description: Education authorities in Tanzania’s Morogoro region have ordered the conversion of a school site back into a garage after the facility failed to meet required standards for operating as an educational...

item.title: Jayne Mansfield's 1954 Fourth of July Portrait with Uncle Sam Top Hat
item.description: This article explores a 1954 holiday-themed photograph of Jayne Mansfield, a rising Hollywood star, posing with an oversized Uncle Sam top hat as part of a patriotic pin-up campaign. The image, set ag...

item.title: Simba SC Wins CRDB Federation Cup After Beating Azam FC
item.description: Simba SC has secured the CRDB Federation Cup title by defeating Azam FC 1-0 in a tightly contested final at Gombani Stadium in Pemba. The victory marks another milestone for Simba, one of Tanzania's m...
